claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for, and if so, how many?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have a child - my son Alex. He's 8 years old. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I can see you have a son, Alex, who is 8 years old.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

Next question: Does Alex live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't live with me at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I understand. Since Alex doesn't live with you at the moment, I need to ask: do you contribute financially towards looking after him? For example, do you pay for things like food, clothes, or pocket money - at least the same amount as Child Benefit would be (which is currently around £25 per week for the eldest/only child)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently contributing financially towards his upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me ask one more important question to complete my assessment:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex (for example, the person he lives with)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm not sure if anyone else is claiming it for him or not.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all the information you've provided. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me, unfortunately you would not be eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex at this time. Let me explain why:

To qualify for Child Benefit, you normally need to be "responsible" for the child. You're usually considered responsible if either:
- You live with the child, OR
- You're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards looking after them - for example on food, clothes, or pocket money

Since Alex doesn't live with you and you're not currently contributing financially towards his upkeep, you wouldn't meet the responsibility test for Child Benefit.

However, it's important to note that only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child. If the person Alex lives with is not currently claiming Child Benefit for him, and you were to start contributing financially to his upkeep (at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit amount), you could potentially become eligible in the future.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or your situation?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, so meets the age requirement (under 16). The claimant lives in the UK, which satisfies the residency requirement. However, the claimant does not meet the 'responsibility test' which is essential for Child Benefit eligibility. Alex does not live with the claimant, and the claimant is not making any financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep. Under the rules, you're usually responsible for a child if either: (1) you live with them, or (2) you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards looking after them. Since neither condition is met, the claimant is not considered responsible for Alex and therefore is not eligible for Child Benefit for him.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (8 years old), and lives in the UK. However, Alex does not live with the claimant and the claimant does not contribute financially to Alex's upkeep. Without meeting either aspect of the responsibility test (living with the child or contributing financially at least the equivalent of Child Benefit), the claimant does not qualify for Child Benefit for Alex.

Since neither condition is met, the claimant is not considered responsible for Alex and therefore is not eligible for Child Benefit for him.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (8 years old), and lives in the UK. However, Alex does not live with the claimant and the claimant does not contribute financially to Alex's upkeep. Without meeting either aspect of the responsibility test (living with the child or contributing financially at least the equivalent of Child Benefit), the claimant does not qualify for Child Benefit for Alex.
